trades.sentiment_summary: No specific news articles about CART stock from the last 24 hours (since Dec 28, 2025, 4 PM UTC) appear in the search results. Available updates include a Dec 29, 2025, forecast from CoinCodex indicating bullish technical sentiment despite a predicted long-term drop and current Fear & Greed Index at 39[1]. Analyst consensus remains a Buy rating with price targets up to $57[2][4].

FFG Partners LLC initiated a new investment in Carlyle Group (CG), purchasing 27,315 shares valued at $1.713 million in Q3 2025, representing 0.5% of their portfolio. CG stock is trading around $61.09, with analyst consensus leaning towards Hold but with a $66.14 target price and some Buy ratings. Recent portfolio company updates include NEP Group's $700 million equity investment and Carlyle's $800 million senior notes issuance, though not from the last 24 hours.

Cwm LLC increased its position in Blackstone Inc. (BX) by 28.6% during the third quarter, signaling institutional confidence. Blackstone recently raised its quarterly dividend to $1.29, annualizing to $5.16 with a 3.3% yield. Analyst consensus remains a 'Moderate Buy' with an average price target of $179.95, above the current price near $155.

BMO Capital raised its price target on JBS stock to $18 from $17 while maintaining an Outperform rating on Monday.[2] Recent stock price history shows a decline to $13.60, down 5.23% as of October 6, 2025.[3] Earnings data from August 2025 indicates positive price movements post-reports, with gains up to 3.63%.[1]

Institutional investors Brookstone Capital Management and Pacer Advisors Inc. increased their positions in Carrier Global (CARR) stock on December 28 and 29, 2025, signaling confidence amid recent earnings.[2][3] Coverage highlights the company's $5 billion share repurchase authorization, dividend increase to $0.24 quarterly, and Q3 2025 EPS beat ($0.67 vs. $0.57 expected), despite revenue decline.[1][2][3] Analyst consensus remains 'Hold' with a $72 price target, above the recent open of $53.59.[2][3]

Omnicom completed a $2.95 billion exchange offer for Interpublic Group (IPG) notes, with 93.7% participation, exchanging $2.76 billion for new Omnicom notes and retiring the rest, as part of the merger closed on November 26, 2025.[2][5] Settlement of the exchange is expected on December 2, 2025, consolidating IPG's debt under Omnicom following European Commission approval.[2][5] A technical analysis article on IPG trends appeared on December 29, 2025, but provided no specific sentiment details.[6]

Marathon Asset Management sold shares of Envista Holdings (NVST) as reported on December 29, 2025, amid a backdrop of recent strong Q3 2025 earnings where the company beat expectations with $670 million in sales (up 9.4% core growth) and adjusted EPS of $0.32.[1][2][4] Analyst consensus remains 'Hold' with a $21.77 price target, though some recent upgrades like Zacks to Strong Buy reflect optimism on earnings prospects.[2][5] No other major news events occurred in the last 24 hours.
